skill.workflow_scope: Triage the open incident queue for the on-call rotation and summarize the live blockers.

toml.workflow_scope: Triage the open incident queue for the on-call rotation and summarize the live blockers.

automation.task_prompt: Run `make codex-daily-triage` to triage the open incident queue for the on-call rotation and summarize the live blockers. Keep the task body focused on the workflow itself; the weekday 09:00 cadence belongs in scheduler configuration, not in the task instructions. Use `fixtures/open_incidents.json` as the sample evidence surface for local dry runs.

decisions.rationale: The skill anchors on the live Codex-facing entrypoint because `docs/ops/daily_triage.md` names `make codex-daily-triage` as the workflow entrypoint, and `Makefile` resolves that target to the active `scripts/triage.py` invocation that triages blocker incidents.

The TOML keeps the same live entrypoint as the skill and records the clean-baseline variant so the generated config reads the current workflow files first instead of inheriting rollback-only helpers or stale draft wording.

The automation maps the weekday scheduler literal from `ops/automation/triage.cron` onto the same live entrypoint while leaving the task prompt focused on triage behavior rather than embedding schedule semantics in the task body.

The note explicitly rejects stale candidates because the clean-baseline variant should not pivot to rollback-only files: the legacy skill says it predates the current migration, the legacy schedule says it is not the source of truth, and `scripts/triage_legacy.py` is preserved for rollback diffs only.

rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because this draft says it predates the current repo migration and keeps the legacy helper only so reviewers can compare old vs. new workflow wording.
Rejected because the note says it pre-dates the current automation and must not be used as the source of truth when mapping the live workflow.
Rejected because the legacy target points at the deprecated helper, while the live Codex-facing workflow is documented as `make codex-daily-triage`.
